content:
Hello,
I am facing an issue. I am trying to back-port Rust (tag = rust-v6.1-rc1) to 5.15.y kernel (arm64).
After resolving other issues and finally I could see rust compiler invoked and was compiled core.o successfully. However, I encountered a build break where it showed error as below:

$(NM) -p --defined-only $< \
    | grep -E ' (T|R|D) ' | cut -d ' ' -f 3 \
    | xargs -Isymbol \
    echo 'EXPORT_SYMBOL_RUST_GPL(symbol);' > $@

xargs: Unknown: "Isymbol"

I have no clue how to resolve this error. Any help/pointers will be welcome.

Thank you,
Sarbojit

The command seems identical to the one we have in the kernel. Is that in the output? Or copied from the Makefile? Also, just to be sure: what do you mean by cross-compiling -- is it at least from a Linux host with GNU xargs? Could you compile successfully rust-v6.1-rc1 without issues?

Hi Miguel,

  1. I could not copy the output hence showed the Makefile code where the issue appeared. Makefile location: kernel/rust/Makefile

  2. Yes, it is Linux host with GNU xargs (ubuntu 22.04 LTS)

  3. Yes, I could successfully compile the v6.1-rc1 without any issue.

To summarise, the problem is in the backport but I am unable to figure out why. I need help to narrow down my search space.

Thank you
Miguel Ojeda has marked this topic as resolved.
